briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate change expert spokesperson briefing for March 18, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis Warnings and Deepening Impacts
sections.content: Several reports released today warn of the severe risks posed by climate change. Domestically, a shocking projection has emerged, indicating that a lack of activity and deteriorating health due to climate change could lead to 500,000 premature deaths annually [1], and a study suggesting that changes in Earth's rotation speed could alter the length of a day has also been presented [2]. Internationally, direct impacts of climate change are being observed in various regions, including record-breaking winter high temperatures in the Western U.S. [98], clustered tornado outbreaks [95], and a decline in chum salmon populations in Japan [107]. Notably, a Lancet study warns that millions worldwide could suffer from insufficient physical activity by 2050 [111], and with even wine production threatened [113], it once again reminds us of climate change's widespread impact across various sectors. The strong message that greenhouse gas emissions must go 'beyond zero' to 'negative' to avert a climate catastrophe [9] further underscores the severity of the current situation.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Education and Lifestyle Practices for Domestic Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: Meanwhile, active movements are being observed domestically for the transition to a carbon-neutral society. In Cheongyang-gun, Chungnam Province, the 'Chungnam Climate and Environment Education Center,' the nation's first residential carbon-neutral education facility, has opened [13, 14, 22, 92], and is expected to serve as a hub for carbon-neutral environmental education. In Gwangju and Gyeongsan cities, 'Green Leaders' and youth supporters, respectively, have been launched to encourage citizens' participation in carbon-neutral practices [15, 19, 60], and various local governments, including Naju City and Imsil-gun, are expanding carbon-neutral forests through citizen-participatory forest creation and afforestation projects [39, 50, 55, 63]. Furthermore, the Korea Environment Corporation is recruiting for its greenhouse gas management specialist training program [21, 32, 54], and KEPCO has launched its energy-saving platform 'Wise Electric Life' service [93], demonstrating diverse efforts to normalize carbon neutrality and enhance expertise.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Renewable Energy Expansion and Policy Challenges
sections.content: In the renewable energy sector, positive developments are emerging alongside policy challenges. While 7 out of 10 citizens expressed willingness to install solar panels [72, 87], the actual participation rate remains at around 10% [68], indicating an urgent need to lower barriers to participation. A bill has been proposed to integrate five power generation companies into a single power generation corporation to expand public-led renewable energy [70, 81], and Woori Financial Group is also showing positive investment trends, such as establishing a regional infrastructure fund in Haenam to expand renewable energy investments [74]. However, it has become evident that global affairs and uncertainties from past policies, such as the phenomenon of reverting to coal use due to LNG supply shocks in Asia [85] and the possibility of compensation for the Trump administration's offshore wind cancellations [84], could act as obstacles to the renewable energy transition. Meanwhile, discussions have begun on improving the compensation system for frequent shutdowns of thermal power plants due to increased renewable energy generation [86, 89], which will be an important step towards stable power grid operation.

sections.title: 🌍 Climate Change Adaptation and Scientific Technology Innovation
sections.content: Adaptation efforts and scientific technology innovations to address the inevitable impacts of climate change are also steadily progressing. The Korea Rural Community Corporation has developed 'K-HAS CS 2.0,' an agricultural facility design system that reflects climate change [4, 10, 12], thereby enhancing adaptability in the agricultural sector. Yeongcheon City is operating a fruit tree pollen bank to support fruit farmers in responding to climate change and declining bee populations [5, 6], and Anseong City hosted training on high-quality rice cultivation techniques in response to climate change [11]. Furthermore, the National Assembly is accelerating the transition to ESG management by strengthening carbon neutrality legislation [49], and Shinhan Bank is leading carbon neutrality through 'green finance' [56], thus solidifying the foundation for climate response in the financial and policy sectors as well. Alongside research into nature's adaptability, such as the possibility that deep-sea microorganisms are adapting to climate change [101], innovative approaches like the introduction of climate crisis insurance [112] are opening new horizons for coexistence in the era of climate change.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted the severe warnings of climate change, alongside the simultaneous domestic and international efforts and scientific approaches being undertaken to overcome it. We ask for your continued interest and action to participate in overcoming the climate crisis. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Hana Financial Investment, located in Yeouido, Seoul. Provided by Hana Financial Investment. Hana Financial Investment announced on the 18th that it has been selected as the entrusted fund manager for the 'Chungnam Corporate Growth Venture Fund (Chungnam Venture Fund)' promoted by Korea Venture Investment. This fund is a 19.1 billion KRW venture fund established to invest in the growth stages of companies in the Chungnam region, and is expected to launch within the second quarter. Through its participation in this fund, Hana Financial Investment plans to invest in areas such as display materials and equipment, eco-friendly automotive parts, and more.
